message:
So my friend texted me at 11 PM last week in an absolute panic because she smelled smoke in her apartment building. Turned out it was just someone grilling on a balcony two floors down, but watching her spiral got me thinking—she's the type to catastrophize, but honestly? That instinct to take it seriously *first* isn't the worst habit.

It actually made me check my own smoke alarms (one battery was dead, whoops), and we ended up talking about escape routes in her place. Nothing dramatic, just "if the front was blocked, what's plan B?" Weirdly, it was kind of helpful to talk through it together without any doom-and-gloom energy.

I think the real win wasn't avoiding a panic—it was turning the moment into something practical. A quick refresh on the basics made us both feel steadier.

Do you have that one friend who worries about everything? Have you two ever accidentally done something smart because of it?
